summary: Brought a batch of previously 0%-match menu (`mn`) code into C, centered on event match, item switch, count, and main-rule menu helpers. The PR also retyped `mn_8022EC18` from a raw `float*` triple to `AnimLoopSettings*` and propagated that through several menu animation callers. decomp.dev reported +2768 matched bytes, +0.07% matched code, 15 new 100% matches, and several partial `mnevent` improvements. The only human review explicitly agreed that `AnimLoopSettings` was the right direction.

key_files.role: Largest source addition; added event-menu implementations, transition procs, text/GObj cleanup, page setup, and archive section loading.
Replaced many placeholder prototypes with typed signatures, added `MnEventData`, and declared event-menu globals.
Changed `mn_8022EC18` to consume `AnimLoopSettings*` and use named `start_frame`, `end_frame`, and `loop_frame` fields.
Updated the `mn_8022EC18` prototype to match the new `AnimLoopSettings*` type.
Filled in `mn_8023164C`, `mn_80231714`, and `mn_80231F80`, including menu-state transitions and rule availability checks.
Created the item-switch header with `MnItemSwData`, prototypes, and `mnItemSw_804D6BEC` extern.
Added `MnCountData` with explicit offsets and typed the newly matched count-menu functions.
Updated existing callers/data declarations for the `mn_8022EC18` `AnimLoopSettings*` signature.

what_changed: `mnevent.c` went from effectively empty to 272 lines of C. New implementations include `mnEvent_8024CE74`, `mnEvent_8024D014`, `mnEvent_8024D0CC`, `mnEvent_8024D4E0`, `mnEvent_8024D7E0`, `fn_8024E1B4`, `fn_8024E2A0`, `fn_8024E34C`, `mnEvent_8024E420`, and `mnEvent_8024E838`.

`mnevent.h` added `MnEventData` with `page`, `first_event`, nine-entry `HSD_GObj*`, `HSD_Text*` text/icon arrays, and `name_text`/`desc_text`; it also replaced most placeholder event-menu prototypes with concrete argument and return types.

`mnitemsw.c` added `mnItemSw_8023405C`, which selects an item JObj by walking child/next links with a split at index 16, and `mnItemSw_802358C0`, which enters menu kind `0x10` and starts the item-switch GObj proc.
`mnitemsw.h` introduced `MnItemSwData` with item bytes, unknown byte fields, and `jobjs[9]`, plus item-switch prototypes and `mnItemSw_804D6BEC`.

`mncount.c` added `mnCount_802513F4`, which toggles JObj hidden flags based on count-menu cursor position, and `mnCount_802517E0`, which initializes cursor/page size and clears label/value text pointers.
`mncount.h` added `MnCountData` with explicit offsets and size `0x5C`, including `labels[10]`, `values[10]`, and `title`.

`mnmainrule.c` implemented `mn_8023164C` and `mn_80231714` menu-entry routines, including `prev_menu`, `cur_menu`, `hovered_selection`, `GObj_Create`, `HSD_GObj_SetupProc`, and `mn_80230E38(mode)` setup; `mn_80231714` additionally sets `mn_804D6BD4 = 1`.
`mnmainrule.c` implemented `mn_80231F80(u8)` as a boolean rule-availability check using `gm_801A4310`, `gmMainLib_8015EE0C`, and `gmMainLib_8015EE44`.
`mn_8022EC18` changed from raw `float*` indexing to `AnimLoopSettings*` field access, while preserving the volatile load of `start_frame` used in `HSD_JObjReqAnimAll`.

Several callers were adjusted for the new animation type: `mnhyaku` and `mnvibration` changed static `f32[3]` animation triples into `AnimLoopSettings`; `mndeflicker`, `mnsound`, and `mnlanguage` updated call sites, with `mnlanguage` using explicit casts where its data still has `x/y`-style fields.

decomp.dev reported 15 new 100% matches: major ones included `mnEvent_8024E838`, `mnEvent_8024E420`, `fn_8024E34C`, `mn_80231714`, `mnEvent_8024D4E0`, `mn_8023164C`, `mnCount_802513F4`, `mnEvent_8024D014`, `fn_8024E2A0`, `mnItemSw_8023405C`, `mn_80231F80`, `mnEvent_8024D0CC`, `mnCount_802517E0`, `mnEvent_8024D7E0`, and `mnItemSw_802358C0`.
decomp.dev also reported partial improvements in `mnevent`, notably `mnEvent_8024CE74` to 99.42% and `fn_8024E1B4` to 50.90%, plus `.data`, `.sdata2`, and `.sdata` improvements.
smart_moves: Recognized that the three-float animation ranges used by menu code are semantically `AnimLoopSettings`, not anonymous `f32[3]`, and updated the shared helper plus multiple callers accordingly.
Kept the volatile `start_frame` load inside `mn_8022EC18`, which is a strong matching clue and avoids changing generated code while improving the type.

Used explicit user-data structs (`MnEventData`, `MnItemSwData`, `MnCountData`) with offset comments and fixed array lengths so GObj user data could be expressed naturally instead of as raw pointer arithmetic.

Matched menu transition patterns by consistently updating `mn_804A04F0.prev_menu`, `cur_menu`, and `hovered_selection`, creating a proc GObj with `GObj_Create(0, 1, 0x80)`, and setting `proc->flags_3 = HSD_GObj_804D783C`.
Used `PAD_STACK` and `FORCE_PAD_STACK_4` only where the new C needed stack-shape help, rather than overcomplicating the higher-level logic.
In `mnEvent_8024E838`, used a `char*` base from `&mnEvent_803EF740` plus explicit offsets for `lbArchive_LoadSections`, a practical data-reference tactic for matching archive-section load calls.
decomp_lessons: When a helper takes a start frame, end frame, and loop frame, try typing the parameter as `AnimLoopSettings*`; this can improve both readability and matching across many menu files.
`loop_frame == -0.1f` is treated specially by `mn_8022EC18`: the function clamps at `end_frame` instead of looping through a new frame range.
Menu GObj user data often starts with small state bytes plus padding, followed by fixed arrays of `HSD_GObj*`, `HSD_Text*`, or `HSD_JObj*`; defining those structs can unlock many nearby functions.
Arrow and page indicator visibility in menu code often compiles cleanly as `lb_80011E24` child lookup followed by `HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll` or `HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll` with `JOBJ_HIDDEN`.

Exit/transition procs frequently clean up `HSD_Text*` objects with `HSD_SisLib_803A5CC4`, unlink child GObjs with `HSD_GObjPLink_80390228`, then replace the proc via `HSD_GObjProc_8038FE24` and `HSD_GObj_SetupProc`.
If one data declaration cannot yet be globally retyped, a local cast to `AnimLoopSettings*` can bridge the shared helper signature while preserving existing declarations, as shown in `mnlanguage.c`.
naming_conventions: Menu subsystem functions keep file-based prefixes such as `mnEvent_`, `mnItemSw_`, `mnCount_`, `mnHyaku_`, `mnDeflicker_`, `mnSoundTest_`, and generic `mn_` for shared menu code.
Unknown callbacks continue to use `fn_<address>` names, e.g. `fn_8024E1B4`, `fn_8024E2A0`, `fn_8024E34C`, and `fn_80233E10`.
Prototype comments retain the address-short form, e.g. `/* 24CE74 */ s32 mnEvent_8024CE74(void);`.

Struct fields use explicit offset comments and preserve unknown names like `pad_01`, `x21`, `x23`, while known fields receive semantic names such as `cursor`, `page_size`, `labels`, `values`, `first_event`, `name_text`, and `desc_text`.
Data/global symbols remained address-based where semantics were not established, e.g. `mnEvent_803EF740`, `mnEvent_804D6C65`, `mnCount_803EFAA0`, and `mn_804A04F0`.
`AnimLoopSettings` field names `start_frame`, `end_frame`, and `loop_frame` are preferred over `arg1[0]`, `arg1[1]`, and `arg1[2]`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `PAD_STACK(8)`, `PAD_STACK(16)`, and `FORCE_PAD_STACK_4` were used in newly matched functions to satisfy stack layout without obscuring control flow.
`mn_8022EC18` retained `HSD_JObjReqAnimAll(arg0, *(volatile float*) &arg1->start_frame)`, indicating a deliberate volatile-load tactic for matching.
Static animation triples in `mnhyaku` and `mnvibration` were converted from `f32[3]` to `AnimLoopSettings`, while call sites pass the struct address directly.

`mnlanguage.c` uses `(AnimLoopSettings*) &mnLanguage_803EF5AC` and `(AnimLoopSettings*) &mnLanguage_803EF5A0`, showing a narrow cast when the underlying layout matches but the declaration has not yet been renamed.
`mnsound.c` changed from a broad `(f32*)` cast to passing `&mnSound_803EEED8[0]`, aligning the call with the new typed helper.

`mnEvent_8024E838` forms archive load label addresses through `char* base = (char*) &mnEvent_803EF740` plus offsets like `base + 0xA0`, `base + 0xB8`, `base + 0xD4`, `base + 0xF4`, and `base + 0x118`.
`mnItemSw_8023405C` matches item JObj selection by branching at `idx >= 16` and walking sibling links with `HSD_JObjGetChild` and `HSD_JObjGetNext`.

review_feedback.author: lukechampine
review_feedback.feedback: The author said the PR targeted remaining files with 0% match and was "a little suspicious" of the `AnimLoopSettings` changes.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Reviewer approved and wrote: "I think `AnimLoopSettings` does make sense."
No inline review comments were present in the slice.
Automated report showed matched code 58.59% (+0.07%, +2768 bytes), 15 new matches, and 5 unmatched-item improvements.

follow_up_queries: Find remaining `mn_8022EC18` callers still using casts or raw float arrays and consider retyping their data as `AnimLoopSettings` where the layout supports it.
Investigate the remaining mismatch in `mnEvent_8024CE74` reported at 99.42%; the source is present but not fully matching according to decomp.dev.
Investigate `fn_8024E1B4`, which was added but only reached 50.90% in the bot report.
Check whether `mnmainrule.h` should type `mn_80231714` as `void mn_80231714(void)`; this diff implemented it but left the header line as `UNK_RET mn_80231714(UNK_PARAMS)`.
Use the new `MnEventData` layout to attack remaining event-menu placeholders such as `mnEvent_8024D15C`, `mnEvent_8024D5B0`, `fn_8024D864`, and `mnEvent_8024E524`.
Refine unknown fields in `MnItemSwData` (`x21`, `x23`, `pad`) and event/menu globals once more neighboring item-switch and event-menu functions are decompiled.
